name: Balkan Vegetable Stew
recipeIngredient: 1 large green pepper, cut into strips
recipeInstructions:
1. In a medium saucepan in hot oil, sauté onions and garlic for 3 minutes.
2. Add broth, salt, thyme, basil, and pepper to the pan.
3. Combine remaining ingredients except Sauce in a large casserole dish (shred cabbage coarsely).
4. Bring the broth mixture to a boil and pour over the vegetables.
5. Cover; bake in a preheated oven (350 degrees) for 30 minutes or until vegetables are tender.
6. Serve hot or cold with Toasted Walnut Sauce. Makes 6 servings.
Per serving: 263 calories, 9 grams protein, 39 grams carbohydrates, 9 grams fat, 3 milligrams cholesterol.
Toasted Walnut Sauce:
1. Soak bread in water to cover until well saturated.
2. In a heavy skillet over medium heat, cook walnuts for 5 to 7 minutes, stirring occasionally, until lightly toasted.
3. Drain bread and squeeze dry.
4. In a blender or food processor, grind walnuts fine.
5. Add bread, salt, pepper, and half the milk. Continue blending until smooth.
6. Gradually add the remaining milk to give a thick yet smooth, pourable sauce.
